#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove constness from the buffer to create a binary_stream
    auto non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>&>(buffer);

    // Create a binary_stream object from the buffer
    hexi::binary_stream<std::span<uint8_t>> stream(non_const_buffer);

    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;

    // Read the message_type
    stream >> message_type;

    // Read the body_length
    stream >> body_length;

    // Skip the body_data
    stream.skip(body_length);

    // Read the footer
    stream >> footer;

    return {message_type, body_length, footer};
}
```

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove constness from the buffer to use with buffer_adaptor (which requires non-const)
    auto non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>>(buffer);

    // Create a buffer_adaptor from the non-const buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    DataResult result;

    // Preview the first byte (data_type) without advancing the read pointer
    uint8_t data_type;
    adaptor.copy(&data_type, 1);
    result.data_type = data_type;

    // Create a binary_stream from the adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Read the item_count (uint32_t) - this advances the read pointer
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream.get(&item_count, 1);

    // Check if the buffer has enough data for the items
    // We have already read 5 bytes (1 for data_type and 4 for item_count)
    // Now we need to read item_count * 4 bytes
    if (adaptor.size() < 5 + item_count * 4) {
        // Not enough data, return empty vectors
        return result;
    }

    // Read the items based on data_type
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        // Integers
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.get(result.int_items.data(), item_count);
    } else {
        // Floats
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.get(result.float_items.data(), item_count);
    }

    return result;
}
```

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    auto non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>>(buffer);
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);
    DataResult result;

    // Preview the first byte (data_type) without advancing the read pointer
    uint8_t data_type;
    adaptor.copy(&data_type, 1);
    result.data_type = data_type;

    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Read the item_count (uint32_t) - this advances the read pointer
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream.get(&item_count, 1);

    // Check if the buffer has enough data for the items
    // We have already read 5 bytes, so we need to read item_count * 4 bytes
    if (stream.read_max() < item_count * 4) {
        // Not enough data, return empty vectors
        return result;
    }

    // Read the items based on data_type
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.get(result.int_items.data(), item_count);
    } else {
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.get(result.float_items.data(), item_count);
    }

    return result;
}
```

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor for the buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);

        return false;

    // Create a binary_stream for writing

    // Write the header and the items
    stream << header;
    stream << items;

}

    // Create a buffer_read_adaptor for the input buffer
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);

    std::vector<uint8_t> result;

    while (adaptor.size() > 0) {
        // Determine the current read chunk size
        std::size_t current_chunk_size = std::min(chunk_size, adaptor.size());

        // Create a temporary buffer to read into
        std::vector<uint8_t> temp(current_chunk_size);

        // Read the data
        adaptor.read(temp.data(), current_chunk_size);

        // Append the temporary buffer to the result
        result.insert(result.end(), temp.begin(), temp.end());
    }

    return result;
}
```

   However, note that the problem does not specify what to do if the buffer is empty initially. The while loop condition checks for that.

   This code should meet the requirements.

   We are using `assert` to check the free space. In a production environment, we might want to handle the case where the buffer is too small, but the problem does not specify.

   Given the exam context, this solution should be acceptable.
